UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile within the NEU Access Hub, including their institutional email, student ID, and assigned access role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The institutional email address of the user.

studentId.description: The student identification number associated with the user.

role.description: The access role assigned to the user, either 'user' or 'admin'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Visit.title: Visit
Visit.description: Records details of a visitor's entry and exit, typically linked to a user profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Visit entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity associated with this visit. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Visit)

checkInTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the visitor checked in.

checkOutTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the visitor checked out. This field is optional if the visitor has not yet checked out.

purpose.description: The purpose or reason for the visit. This field is optional.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the visit record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the visit record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) must match the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes denormalized 'role' field for authorization independence and 'studentId' for login linkage.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Records individual visits associated with a specific user. The 'userId' is inherited from the parent path and also explicitly stored in the document for robust ownership and queryability, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made the visit, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: visitId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific visit record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for the NEU Access Hub prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs) by leveraging a hierarchical, path-based ownership model combined with essential data denormalization. 

1.  **Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**
    *   **UserProfile:** User profiles are stored in `/user_profiles/{userId}`. The `role` field (either 'user' or 'admin') is denormalized directly into each `UserProfile` document. This is critical for authorization independence because security rules can directly evaluate `get(/user_profiles/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role` without relying on `get()` calls to a parent or unrelated document for role-based access. This ensures atomic operations and simplifies rule debugging. The user's `studentId` and `email` are also part of this document, allowing for direct authentication checks within the profile itself.
    *   **Visit:** Visit records are stored as a subcollection under the respective user's profile: `/user_profiles/{userId}/visits/{visitId}`. The `userId` is present both in the document path (as a wildcard) and explicitly within the `Visit` document. This explicit `userId` field in the `Visit` document adheres to the denormalization mandate for subcollections. If access to a `Visit` document were to depend on any attribute of its parent `UserProfile` *other than* the `userId` in its path, that attribute would also be denormalized into the `Visit` document. In this current schema, the `userId` is sufficient for ownership checks.

2.  **QAPs (Query-Aware Security Rules):**
    *   **Structural Segregation:** Each collection (`user_profiles`, `visits`) holds homogeneous data with consistent security requirements. There are no mixed public/private documents within the same collection, simplifying `list` rules.
    *   **Path-Based Ownership:** By using `/user_profiles/{userId}` and `/user_profiles/{userId}/visits/{visitId}`, the structure inherently supports QAPs for user-specific data. When a regular user queries for their profile or their visits, the `userId` in the query path (`/user_profiles/$(request.auth.uid)`) naturally scopes the result to only their owned data. The security rules then only need to verify that `request.auth.uid == userId` (for users) or check for an admin role (for broader access). This prevents rules from acting as filters, which is inefficient and insecure. An admin could be allowed to `list` all documents in `/user_profiles` or `/user_profiles/{userId}/visits` (for any `userId`), with the rule explicitly checking `request.auth.uid`'s role via their `UserProfile` document.

This design ensures simple, robust, and easily debuggable security rules by making authorization context explicit in the data structure and minimizing complex cross-document `get()` calls.
